name: engulfment
title: Engulfment
question: Does an initially half-light/half-dark aggregate engulf to the same sorted configuration as the random-IC sort?
hypothesis: Global energy minimization drives light to engulf dark independent of initial condition; engulfment is slow (~10^4 MCS).
objective: Simulate the sorting energies from a top-light/bottom-dark IC to 10000 MCS and measure homotypic and heterotypic fractional lengths to observe engulfment.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 18+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=14, J_dd=2, J_ld=11, J_lM=16, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+3, gamma_lM=+9, gamma_dM=+15); T=10.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: half_split. Figures reproduced: Fig 18, Fig 19.
claim: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).
expected_behavior: Light forms an outer layer over dark by 10000 MCS
Light-medium fraction rises while dark-medium falls to ~0

findings.statement: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Engulfment example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 18, Fig 19.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

derived.insight: Light cells engulf the dark mass and the final state matches the random-IC sort, confirming initial-condition independence (Figs 18-19).
